overview: Izzet Steel-Cutter is a fast, aggressive Modern deck that leverages turn-one mana rocks to deploy Cori-Steel Cutter early and apply relentless pressure. By combining Mox Opal and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student, the deck accelerates its curve while maintaining card advantage to outpace opponents. The goal is to establish a threatening board presence before opponents can stabilize or generate value.
win_conditions: Early aggressive attacks with Cori-Steel Cutter
Mana acceleration via Mox Opal and Tamiyo to outpace opponents
Card advantage from Tamiyo to maintain board presence

keep_criteria: Hands with Mox Opal or Tamiyo plus Cori-Steel Cutter
Two mana rocks with a one-mana spell
Keep any 7-card hand with 2+ lands (including Mox Opal) and at least one of Tamiyo or Cori-Steel Cutter.
Keep hands with Mox Opal plus two artifacts in play or in hand if you have a turn-2 play.
mulligan_criteria: Hands without Cori-Steel Cutter or early threats
Mulligan hands with three or more lands and no early threats, or hands with Mox Opal but no artifacts to enable it.

early_game: Play Mox Opal or Tamiyo on turn one
Cast Cori-Steel Cutter on turn two or three
Turn 1: Play a land, then Mox Opal if possible.
If you have a two-drop artifact or creature, play it to enable Opal.
Turn 2: Cast Tamiyo or Cori-Steel Cutter.
If you have Opal plus two artifacts, you can cast a three-mana spell on turn two.
Sequence lands to ensure you can hit your mana steps without flooding
mid_game: Use Tamiyo's card draw to find answers or additional threats
Maintain aggressive tempo to prevent opponent stabilization
Preserve mana for interaction until turn three or four.
Use Tamiyo's abilities to cycle cards and maintain hand size.
Do not overcommit artifacts if the board is unstable
Opal enables spells, but do not sacrifice your hand for tempo.
The graveyard is generally neutral, so avoid self-mill unless explicitly synergistic.
Commit Cori-Steel Cutter when you have protection or can trade favorably.
late_game: Leverage accumulated card advantage to outvalue opponents
Scale board presence with additional threats
Close out games with relentless attacks
Cori-Steel Cutter with protection or haste, backed by Tamiyo's card advantage and Opal's mana acceleration.
Overwhelming board presence with artifact synergies and direct damage spells.
When you have three or more mana available, Tamiyo on the board, and the opponent has no sweepers or counterspells in hand.
Also commit when you can trade threats favorably or when the opponent is at low life with no blockers.

key_cards.name: Mox Opal
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to enable turn-two Cori-Steel Cutter or other red spells

key_cards.name: Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student
key_cards.notes: Deploy early to draw into answers or additional threats while fixing mana

key_cards.name: Cori-Steel Cutter
key_cards.notes: Attack as soon as it enters to maximize pressure and trigger its abilities

sequencing_tips: Play Mox Opal before Tamiyo to ensure red mana availability for Cori-Steel Cutter
Attack with Cori-Steel Cutter immediately upon entering the battlefield
Hold up mana for potential interaction if the opponent plays defensively
Prioritize playing mana rocks before spells to maximize turn-one and turn-two impact
Mox Opal requires two artifacts to activate, so do not play it on turn one unless you have a backup plan.
Tamiyo's abilities likely scale with artifacts or provide card draw
Cori-Steel Cutter's name suggests cutting or combat damage
protect it with counters or cheap artifacts.

matchups.advice: Apply relentless early pressure before they stabilize
Use Tamiyo's card draw to find answers and race
Leverage mana rocks to outvalue their curve

Use split spells, cheap artifacts to enable Opal, and Tamiyo's card draw to out-value counters. Do not overcommit to one spell; threaten with multiple small plays and force them to use multiple counters.
Apply early pressure with Cori-Steel Cutter. Use artifact hate and spot removal to slow their mana development. Tamiyo's interaction is key to disrupting their acceleration steps.

Win the card advantage war. Tamiyo's abilities are crucial for cycling and drawing into answers. Prioritize keeping interaction in hand. Trade artifacts favorably and use Cori-Steel Cutter to close when they run out of answers. Value each spell carefully.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers like Cyclonic Rift or Wrath of God, counterspells like Spell Pierce or Mana Leak, and targeted removal like Shock or Path.
Track opponent artifact count if they are also artifact-based.
Note when they tap out for a single big spell or when they are low on resources for interaction.
